title: Imperial IDE OPS integration, bugfix, task console activation, and taskpack builder wiring on PC
mission_summary: Integrate the bundled IMPERIUM_IDE_OPERATIONAL_V0_1 candidate engine into the current Imperial IDE. Patch known example bugs, preserve the correct OPS/ENGINE structure, add Astronomicon-compatible taskpack generation, wire Task Console, Taskpack Builder, Launch Card, Lifecycle, Receipts, and Git Closure into GUI/TUI/CLI, validate, commit, and push.

strategic_intent.kernel: Imperium New Reality is the kernel.
strategic_intent.imperial_ide: Imperial IDE must become the place where the Owner creates, registers, launches, tracks, validates, and closes tasks.
strategic_intent.ops: OPS is the operational task lifecycle engine: task console, builder, registration, launch card, dry-run lifecycle, receipts, bundle gate, and git closure.
strategic_intent.mechanicus: Mechanicus remains the tool and command validator. OPS must not bypass Mechanicus safety policy.
strategic_intent.astronomicon: Astronomicon remains the canonical task admission and registry authority.
strategic_intent.administratum: Administratum owns bundle gates, continuity, receipts, and closure.
strategic_intent.owner_goal: Stop hand-building taskpacks manually; begin creating tasks from inside Imperial IDE.

source_context_to_consider: Owner decision: integrate OPS and fix known bugs, keep unsafe execution blocked

organ_route.DOCTRINARIUM: OPS doctrine, Astronomicon taskpack schema compatibility, no overclaim boundaries
organ_route.OFFICIO_AGENTIS: owner-facing Russian final summary and usage guide
organ_route.ASTRONOMICON: live registration path, taskpack admission compatibility, launch card, registry
organ_route.ADMINISTRATUM: receipts, bundle gate, lifecycle closure, git push receipt, continuity
organ_route.MECHANICUS: policy precheck, dry-run gateway, validation center, command receipt safety
organ_route.INQUISITION: fake-green trap, unsafe execution block, schema validation downgrade
organ_route.STRATEGIUM: operator flow, task templates, next build ladder, KPD of inside-IDE tasking
organ_route.SCHOLA_IMPERIALIS: workflow lessons, task template library, reusable patterns

bundled_binary_archives.note: Bundled ZIPs are candidate payload archives. Their internal language is handled during integration and not treated as taskpack root policy text.

cyrillic_in_taskpack.scope: MANIFEST.json, TASK_SPEC.md, ACCEPTANCE_GATES.md, OUTPUT_REQUIREMENTS.md, TASK_ROUTE_MANIFEST_TEMPLATE.json, TASK_START_ACK_TEMPLATE.json

binary_bundles_exception.reason: Binary source archives are candidate payloads and are inventoried by receipts.

localization_exception.scope: owner-facing runtime files only after Officio role entry

allowed_when: source archive hash and inventory are recorded
OPS is imported under ORGANS/IMPERIAL_IDE/OPS only
known OPS bugs are fixed or exact blockers are recorded
Astronomicon-compatible taskpack generation smoke passes or exact blocker is recorded
GUI/TUI/CLI integrations smoke pass or exact blockers are recorded
created or modified Python files compile
no secret files or local operator configs are staged
forbidden_push_cases: failed validation without explicit BLOCK verdict
secrets, credentials, or local operator configs staged
destructive cleanup or delete without owner batch approval
live LLM backend enabled with secrets

primary_outputs: Imperial IDE CLI commands for task console and lifecycle
Workbench panel wiring for Task Console and Taskpack Builder
Live or dry-run Astronomicon registration mode with receipts
Safety receipts, validation receipts, commit and push
